title: ilex aquifolium subf. macrophylla
intro: Ilex aquifolium subf. macrophylla, also known as the large-leaved holly, is a sub-fossil subgroup of the holly plant (Ilex aquifolium), characterized by its large, dark green leaves and red berries. The leaves and berries of the Ilex aquifolium subf. macrophylla plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ilex aquifolium subf. macrophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of Ilex aquifolium subf. macrophylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the leaves and berries, which can be used to promote overall well-being and health.
